name: Stuffed Tomatoes with Spinach and Yogurt Cheese
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs - basil, oregano, thyme
10 ounces frozen spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Cut off the stem end of each tomato, leaving the cap intact. Discard the pulp and invert the tomatoes on a plate to drain.
3. In a nonstick saucepan, melt butter over low heat.
4. Add green onions and garlic; sauté until soft.
5. Stir in flour, Parmesan cheese, breadcrumbs, and herbs. Cook for 1 minute.
6. Add spinach, Yogurt Cheese, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and cayenne to taste. Mix well.
7. Stuff the spinach mixture into the tomatoes and top with optional Parmesan if using.
8. Store stuffed tomatoes and their caps up to 3 days in advance.
9. Place the tomatoes in an ovenproof serving dish and bake until heated through, about 10 minutes.
10. Top each tomato with its cap and bake for an additional 5 minutes.
11. Serve hot.
Yield: 4 servings
